results.label: Confident — GDPR hiring AI, EU
results.input: We are building an AI tool that screens job applicants and ranks them by suitability. We operate in Germany and France.

domains.reasoning: Applicant screening necessarily processes personal data (privacy) and explicitly uses AI to rank candidates (ai).

jurisdictions.reasoning: Stated operations in Germany and France, both EU members subject to GDPR and local employment law.

data_types.reasoning: Job applicant screening inherently processes behavioural data (resume, interview responses, work history, assessment results).

sector.reasoning: Explicitly described as an applicant screening and ranking tool.

results.label: Confident — US health app
results.input: Our iOS app lets patients in the United States track chronic conditions, log medications, and share data with their doctors.

domains.reasoning: App processes personal health data (privacy domain) and communicates over network/APIs with doctors (cyber domain); no AI/ML mentioned.

jurisdictions.reasoning: Explicitly stated as serving patients in the United States.

data_types.reasoning: Core functionality is tracking chronic conditions and medications, which are health data by definition.

sector.reasoning: App is explicitly designed for patient use and doctor communication, making it a healthcare sector application.

results.label: Confident — UK fintech credit scoring
results.input: We provide algorithmic credit scoring to UK banks. Our model uses transaction history and behavioural signals to approve or decline loan applications.

domains.reasoning: Processing personal financial data with an algorithmic model necessarily involves both privacy and AI domains.

jurisdictions.reasoning: Explicitly stated as serving UK banks.

data_types.reasoning: Transaction history is financial data; behavioural signals are explicitly mentioned as part of the scoring model.

sector.reasoning: Credit scoring for loan approval is a core financial services function.

results.label: Confident — children's education platform, US
results.input: We run an online learning platform for K-12 students in the United States. We collect names, grades, usage data, and video recordings of tutoring sessions.

domains.reasoning: The platform collects and processes personal data (names, grades, usage data, video recordings) of K-12 students.

jurisdictions.reasoning: Platform explicitly operates in the United States serving K-12 students.

data_types.reasoning: Platform serves K-12 (children); collects usage data (behavioural); and collects names and video recordings (general_pi).

sector.reasoning: Online learning platform explicitly serving K-12 educational institutions and students.

results.label: Confident — EU smart city surveillance
results.input: Our computer vision platform processes CCTV footage across public spaces in Amsterdam and Brussels to monitor crowd density and detect incidents in real time.

domains.reasoning: Computer vision processes video data of people in public spaces (privacy), uses AI/ML algorithms (ai), and involves networked CCTV infrastructure with real-time data transmission (cyber).

jurisdictions.reasoning: Operations explicitly stated in Amsterdam and Brussels, both in EU member states (Netherlands and Belgium), triggering GDPR and national surveillance law regimes.

data_types.reasoning: CCTV footage in public spaces captures facial/gait biometrics (biometric), real-time crowd positioning (location), and movement patterns and incident behaviour (behavioural).

sector.reasoning: Crowd monitoring and incident detection in public spaces is typically a local/municipal government function, though private security operators are also possible; no explicit sector stated so confidence is medium.
